title: ObjectBox

sections.markdown: ObjectBox is a very fast, native embedded object database (C++ core) with clients in many languages (Dart/Flutter, Java/Kotlin, Swift, Go). Objects in, objects out — no ORM mapping code.

ObjectBox is the choice when you need **raw local speed** with strongly-typed objects and no SQL boilerplate. Its optional Sync product is a differentiator for teams building offline-first collaborative apps.
Ignoring the Store lifecycle: opening/closing repeatedly leaks resources. Open once, close on app termination. Also: forgetting to run codegen after entity changes.
Index only what you query. Use lazy loading for large ToMany relations. Version-tag your data model when you migrate.

flashcards.back: C++, with client bindings in Dart, Java/Kotlin, Swift, Go, etc.
ToOne<T> and ToMany<T> in the entity class.
Very fast writes + an optional multi-device Sync product for offline-first.

options: Local offline-first apps needing speed + optional sync
quiz.explain: Native local DB with an optional Sync product.

challenges.text: Define a User entity, put and find some objects.
Add a ToMany relation and query across it.
Benchmark ObjectBox vs Isar for 100k inserts.
Prototype ObjectBox Sync across two clients.

interviewQuestions: What is ObjectBox and its differentiator?
How do you handle data-model migrations?
